content.text: Implemented parity between `.resolves` and `.rejects` by allowing `.resolves` to accept either: - a `Promise`, **or** - a **function returning a Promise** (which is invoked and unwrapped internally). ### Changes made - **Fix:** `packages/expect/src/index.ts` (`makeResolveMatcher`) now unwraps `actual` when it’s a function, mirroring `.rejects` behavior. - **Test:** Updated existing `.resolves` test in `packages/expect/src/__tests__/matchers.test.js` to include `expect(() => Promise.resolve(...)).resolves...` and `expect(async () => ...).resolves...`. - **Cleanup:** No extra temporary test files remain (the added coverage was merged into existing tests). ### Verification - Ran `.resolves` tests and updated snapshots; tests pass: - `node ./packages/jest/bin/jest.js packages/expect/src/__tests__/matchers.test.js -t "\\.resolves" --updateSnapshot`
